req.title: Data retention
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-020 mandates that expired resources are purged by a nightly cron job and that an audit trail is written. Three primary risk areas emerge: (1) normal expiry path where resources past their retention date are removed and audited (EP positive), (2) a resource exactly at the retention boundary — expired by exactly one second vs still valid by one second (BVA boundary), and (3) a policy mismatch scenario where the job encounters a resource whose retention metadata is corrupt or missing, triggering an incident (NEG negative). A fourth case covers the state transition from 'active' to 'purged' to confirm the resource is no longer retrievable after the job runs (STC positive). Each test seeds its own data via fixtures, calls /cron/retention, and asserts both the resource state and the audit log entry, ensuring idempotency by cleaning up created resources regardless of outcome.

tests.name: test_expired_resource_is_purged_and_audited
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A resource exists whose retention_expires_at is at least 24 hours in the past
Audit log is accessible via GET /audit
actions: Seed an expired resource via <seed_expired_resource> fixture
POST /cron/retention to trigger the nightly job
expected: Audit log contains an entry with action=PURGED for the resource
trace: R-HLT-020: expired resources shall be purged
R-HLT-020: full audit trail shall be written
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture()
def expired_resource():
    resource = <seed_expired_resource>(
        retention_expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=2)
    )
    yield resource
    <cleanup_resource>(resource["id"])

def test_expired_resource_is_purged_and_audited(expired_resource):
    rid = expired_resource["id"]

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention",
                         headers=<auth_headers>())
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{rid}",
                            headers=<auth_headers>())
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404

    audit = requests.get(f"{BASE}/audit",
                         params={"resource_id": rid},
                         headers=<auth_headers>()).json()
    entries = [e for e in audit["entries"] if e["action"] == "PURGED"]
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    assert entries[0]["resource_id"] == rid

tests.name: test_resource_expired_by_one_second_is_purged
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A resource exists whose retention_expires_at equals now minus 1 second
actions: Seed a resource expiring exactly 1 second before job trigger via <seed_expired_resource>
Seed a second resource expiring exactly 1 second after now (still valid)
GET /resources/{valid_resource_id} to confirm it is retained
expected: Resource expired by 1 s is purged (404)
Resource valid by 1 s is retained (200)
Audit log contains PURGED only for the expired resource
trace: R-HLT-020: resources shall be retained per policy
R-HLT-020: expired resources shall be purged — boundary at expiry timestamp
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture()
def boundary_resources():
    just_expired = <seed_expired_resource>(
        retention_expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
    )
    still_valid = <seed_expired_resource>(
        retention_expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=1)
    )
    yield just_expired, still_valid
    <cleanup_resource>(just_expired["id"])
    <cleanup_resource>(still_valid["id"])

def test_resource_expired_by_one_second_is_purged(boundary_resources):
    expired, valid = boundary_resources
    headers = <auth_headers>()

    requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=headers)

    assert requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/resources/{expired['id']}", headers=headers
    ).status_code == 404

    assert requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/resources/{valid['id']}", headers=headers
    ).status_code == 200

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/audit",
        params={"resource_id": expired["id"]},
        headers=headers
    ).json()
    purged = [e for e in audit["entries"] if e["action"] == "PURGED"]
    assert len(purged) >= 1

tests.name: test_missing_retention_policy_raises_incident
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A resource exists with retention_policy field set to null or missing
Incident management endpoint GET /incidents is accessible
actions: Seed a resource with no retention_policy via <seed_resource_no_policy>
GET /incidents to confirm an incident was raised for the policy mismatch
Verify the resource is NOT purged (retained in safe state)
expected: POST /cron/retention returns HTTP 200 (job completed with partial errors)
GET /incidents returns at least one incident referencing the resource and reason=POLICY_MISMATCH
GET /resources/{resource_id} returns HTTP 200 — resource is not silently deleted
trace: R-HLT-020: incident raised on policy mismatch
R-HLT-020: audit trail integrity — missing policy must not silently suppress action
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture()
def no_policy_resource():
    resource = <seed_resource_no_policy>()
    yield resource
    <cleanup_resource>(resource["id"])
    <cleanup_incident_by_resource>(resource["id"])

def test_missing_retention_policy_raises_incident(no_policy_resource):
    rid = no_policy_resource["id"]
    headers = <auth_headers>()

    cron_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=headers)
    assert cron_resp.status_code == 200

    resource_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/resources/{rid}", headers=headers
    )
    assert resource_resp.status_code == 200, \
        "Resource without policy must not be silently purged"

    incidents = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/incidents",
        params={"resource_id": rid},
        headers=headers
    ).json()
    matched = [
        i for i in incidents["items"]
        if i.get("reason") == "POLICY_MISMATCH"
        and i.get("resource_id") == rid
    ]
    assert len(matched) >= 1, \
        f"Expected POLICY_MISMATCH incident for resource {rid}"

tests.name: test_resource_transitions_active_to_purged_state
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A resource in ACTIVE state exists with an expired retention date
Resource lifecycle states are: ACTIVE -> PENDING_PURGE -> PURGED
actions: Seed an ACTIVE resource with expired retention via <seed_expired_resource>
Verify resource state is ACTIVE before job runs
GET /resources/{resource_id} to confirm final PURGED or 404 state
GET /audit to confirm state transition entries are present
expected: Audit log contains both PENDING_PURGE and PURGED transition entries in order
trace: R-HLT-020: resources shall be purged by nightly job
R-HLT-020: full audit trail — state transitions must be recorded
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture()
def active_expired_resource():
    resource = <seed_expired_resource>(
        state="ACTIVE",
        retention_expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=48)
    )
    yield resource
    <cleanup_resource>(resource["id"])

def test_resource_transitions_active_to_purged_state(active_expired_resource):
    rid = active_expired_resource["id"]
    headers = <auth_headers>()

    pre = requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{rid}", headers=headers).json()
    assert pre["state"] == "ACTIVE"

    requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=headers)

    post = requests.get(f"{BASE}/resources/{rid}", headers=headers)
    assert post.status_code in (404, 200)
    if post.status_code == 200:
        assert post.json()["state"] == "PURGED"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/audit", params={"resource_id": rid}, headers=headers
    ).json()
    actions = [e["action"] for e in audit["entries"]]
    assert "PURGED" in actions
    purged_idx = actions.index("PURGED")
    if "PENDING_PURGE" in actions:
        assert actions.index("PENDING_PURGE") < purged_idx
